label: SES-048 workstream entity schema designed: 1 session, 6 decisions (DEC-123..128), 0 planning items, 7 references

session.title: Workstream entity schema designed; first per-entity governance schema specification produced
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai schema-design conversation for the workstream entity — first per-entity schema-design conversation in the governance entity schema-design workstream. Opened against PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/schema-design-kickoff-workstream.md (the kickoff committed as part of SES-047's artifacts at 369f32e on 05-20-26). The conversation produced one deliverable per the kickoff and the schema-spec methodology guide's template: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/governance-schema-specs/workstream.md (393 lines, committed at b981c1f), creating the governance-schema-specs/ directory in the same commit. Architectural discussion was driven by the operating preferences for ARCHITECTURE mode (surface only decisions that pass the two-part test of real downstream impact and meaningfully different viable options; decide routine items as drafts; consequential decisions use the eight-element template). Four consequential decisions were surfaced and resolved via the template; five routine items were drafted and confirmed in two batches. The conversation also surfaced two pre-existing drifts between the kickoff prompt's working assumptions and DEC-120's explicit decision text — both resolved in favour of DEC-120 (foundation decisions are binding unless cause to override). No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Design the `workstream` entity type schema for V2's storage layer. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces one deliverable: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/governance-schema-specs/workstream.md — the complete schema specification per the template in governance-entity-schema-spec-guide.md. The directory does not yet exist; this conversation creates it. Cadence matches the methodology workstream's schema-design conversations and the user-interface planning conversations: structured architectural discussion driven one decision at a time, building toward the specification section by section. At conversation close: decisions written via direct API as decision records; any deferred items written as planning items; one session record written by Doug through the V2 desktop New Session dialog per the session-record-at-close pattern."

Topics worked through:

1. Pre-flight orientation. Sparse-cloned the crmbuilder repo and read the eight-item reading list named in the kickoff: crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md, governance-schema-workstream-plan.md, governance-entity-schema-spec-guide.md, the six foundation decisions DEC-117..122 from db-export/decisions.json, SES-046 and SES-047 from db-export/sessions.json, multi-engagement-architecture.md, crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/vocab.py, and methodology-schema-specs/domain.md as the structural exemplar. API health check (against the conversation's sandbox localhost, not Doug's machine) and pytest run (sparse checkout doesn't include tests/) were flagged as not meaningfully runnable in the conversation environment; design-only conversation, so neither gates.

2. Two concerns surfaced before opening Decision 1. (a) The kickoff's working assumption for workstream-to-conversation linkage was 'direct foreign key on conversation table,' but DEC-120's decision text explicitly says 'Conversations belong to a workstream via a relationship in the references table.' Drift; DEC-120 binds. (b) The kickoff said three statuses (planned, in_flight, complete) with cancelled and superseded flagged as open, while DEC-120 itself names four statuses including cancelled, and DEC-117 references 'three-status lifecycle posture' as the inherited methodology pattern. Drift; DEC-120 binds for cancelled (in by default), superseded genuinely open.

3. Decision 1 — workstream-to-conversation relationship via references-table edge. Eight-element template. Confirmed DEC-120's references-edge mechanism over the kickoff's working-assumption foreign-key. Cross-spec precedent locked: references-edge over foreign-key for parent-child governance relationships. New relationship kind named: `conversation_belongs_to_workstream` (source-first per DEC-048). Approved Option B.

4. Decision 2 — status lifecycle, five values including superseded. Eight-element template. Confirmed cancelled per DEC-120 with no debate. Resolved superseded as in (five statuses total) for parity with DEC-119's conversation entity (which has superseded as a status), with truly-terminal terminal states (no transitions out of any terminal; reactivation is conceptually a new workstream). Supersession reuses the existing generic `supersedes` reference kind for the (workstream, workstream) pair; access-layer rule requires an outgoing supersedes edge when status is superseded. Approved Option A.

5. Identifier prefix — drafted as WS, not surfaced as a separate decision. Two-letter form acceptable per the spec guide's 2-5 letter range; matches PI's short-prefix posture; doesn't conflict with the existing collision list. Downstream conversations have their own first refusal on their working prefixes.

6. Decision 3 — nested workstreams. Eight-element template. Resolved the deferred-from-DEC-120 question: flat catalog, no nesting in this release. Zero observed need in the project's operating history; DEC-120's 'if nested workstreams are admitted' phrasing leans this way; user-interface cost of hierarchical rendering is real; the retrofit path is documented as a references-edge addition (not self-FK column) so the workstream table is not touched if nesting becomes needed later. Cleveland Business Mentors Planning Item 001 (sub-domain hierarchy on the methodology `domain` entity) is NOT pre-decided by this — the methodology workstream's PI-001 conversation makes its own call with this conversation's reasoning available but not binding. Approved Option A.

7. Decision 4 — lifecycle timestamps. Eight-element template. Per-status lifecycle timestamps adopted: workstream_started_at, _completed_at, _cancelled_at, _superseded_at (four columns, all nullable, server-set on the matching transition). No planned_at column (redundant with created_at). Diverges from the methodology entities' base-timestamps-only pattern; the divergence is appropriate because methodology entities have propose-verify lifecycles while workstream has a workflow-shaped lifecycle that benefits from queryable per-status timestamps. Cross-spec precedent locked: per-status lifecycle timestamps for workflow-shaped governance lifecycles (conversation entity likely inherits this). Approved Option B.

8. Routine items drafted and approved as a single batch — master plan linkage (references-edge to a reference_book record with new kind `workstream_planned_in_reference_book`, provisional naming, reference_book conversation may refine, build-planning reconciles); soft-delete posture (default with restore — workstream is organizing metadata not transactional fact, so append-only is inappropriate; soft-delete is distinct from cancelled status, which is lifecycle outcome rather than administrative removal); API surface (standard 8-endpoint set with no deviations); user-interface considerations (default layout with two natural additions documented in the spec — conditional lifecycle-timestamp rendering on the detail pane showing only non-null timestamps, and supersession-requires-edge UX where the user must add the outgoing supersedes edge before committing status to superseded).

9. Spec drafting in one pass per spec guide section 4.3. governance-schema-specs/workstream.md drafted following the structural shape of methodology-schema-specs/domain.md, with all nine required subsections of section 3 substantively filled. 16 testable acceptance criteria captured (spec guide gate requires 10). Section 3.8 categorized by build / backfill / future tags per the spec guide convention. Section 3.9 cross-references DEC-117..122 as foundation decisions and DEC-046, DEC-048, DEC-013, DEC-115/116 as inherited conventions. Committed at b981c1f after a small fix to the acceptance-criterion-1 column count (10 → 13, correcting the field-section sum).

10. Close-out artifacts authored. After Doug's confirmation, this payload (ses_048.json) and the companion apply prompt (CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-048.md) were drafted in one pass. Identifier check at close-out: latest applied SES is SES-047 so SES-048 is next-available; latest DEC is DEC-122 so DEC-123..128 is next-available for the six decisions; no new planning items (PI-022 already covers retroactive backfill including this workstream's records).
session.artifacts_produced:
Two files committed to PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ in the crmbuilder repo during the conversation, plus three more produced at close:

During the conversation:

(1) governance-schema-specs/workstream.md (new, v1.0, 393 lines, committed at b981c1f). First per-entity schema specification in the governance entity schema-design workstream. Conforms to governance-entity-schema-spec-guide.md. Establishes the workstream entity type for V2 storage with nine fields plus seven timestamps (three inherited base + four per-status lifecycle), five-status lifecycle with truly terminal terminals and supersession-requires-edge rule, no foreign-key columns (parent-child relationships go through refs per Decision 1), references-edge master plan linkage to reference_book with new kind workstream_planned_in_reference_book, references-edge supersession to another workstream reusing the existing generic supersedes kind, standard 8-endpoint API surface, default UI layout with conditional lifecycle-timestamp rendering. Sixteen testable acceptance criteria captured. Vocabulary additions named for build-planning aggregation: REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS += {conversation_belongs_to_workstream, workstream_planned_in_reference_book}; ENTITY_TYPES += {workstream, conversation, reference_book}; matching _kinds_for_pair clauses; matching Alembic migration on refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint.

(2) governance-schema-specs/ directory created in the same commit (b981c1f) as workstream.md. The directory is the home for the remaining five governance entity schema specifications.

At conversation close:

(3) close-out-payloads/ses_048.json (new — this payload). One session (SES-048), six decisions (DEC-123..128), zero planning items, seven references (six decided_in plus one is_about SES-048 → PI-022).

(4) prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-048.md (new — this payload's apply prompt). Idempotent apply targeting the CRMBUILDER engagement.

Via this payload's apply prompt, the following records land in the CRMBUILDER engagement database:

(5) DEC-123 — workstream identifier prefix and format (WS, WS-NNN, two-letter form acceptable without locking the cross-spec norm).

(6) DEC-124 — workstream-to-conversation via references-table edge per DEC-120's decision text; new relationship kind conversation_belongs_to_workstream; cross-spec precedent of references-edge over foreign-key for parent-child governance relationships.

(7) DEC-125 — workstream lifecycle: five statuses including superseded with truly terminal terminals and supersession-requires-edge rule reusing the existing generic supersedes kind.

(8) DEC-126 — workstream field inventory with per-status lifecycle timestamps; cross-spec precedent of per-status lifecycle timestamps for workflow-shaped lifecycles.

(9) DEC-127 — workstream flat, no nesting in this release; retrofit path documented as a references-edge addition rather than self-FK column.

(10) DEC-128 — workstream master plan linkage via references-edge with new kind workstream_planned_in_reference_book; standard 8-endpoint API surface with no deviations; default UI layout with conditional lifecycle-timestamp rendering; default soft-delete posture; sixteen acceptance criteria captured.

(11) Six decided_in references (DEC-123..128 → SES-048) plus one is_about reference (SES-048 → PI-022, mirroring SES-047's pattern of linking the schema-design conversation to the retroactive-backfill planning item).

No source code modified — this is a planning conversation producing schema specifications only. Vocabulary changes, schema migrations, and access-layer methods are aggregated by the build-planning conversation after all six schemas exist.
session.in_flight_at_end: Next workstream conversation: conversation entity schema design (second per-entity schema-design conversation, of six). Opens against PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/schema-design-kickoff-conversation.md. That conversation produces governance-schema-specs/conversation.md and inherits the three cross-spec precedents locked by SES-048: references-edge over foreign-key for parent-child governance relationships, per-status lifecycle timestamps for workflow-shaped lifecycles, and terminal-states-are-terminal discipline. The conversation entity is structurally the workstream entity's most direct downstream consumer — every conversation record will carry an outbound conversation_belongs_to_workstream edge to its workstream per the relationship kind named in this conversation's decisions. After conversation.md lands, the remaining four schema-design conversations follow in workstream order (reference book, work ticket, close-out payload, deposit event). After all six schema specifications exist, the build-planning conversation opens against governance-schema-build-planning-kickoff.md (to be drafted at the close of the deposit-event conversation per SES-047's in-flight notes) and produces the integrating Product Requirements Document, implementation plan, per-slice build prompts, and the consolidated vocab.py update aggregating relationship-kind additions from all six specs. Multi-tenancy routing fix slice B and the Cleveland Business Mentors redo Phase 1 conversation continue in parallel as independent workstreams per DEC-122.

decisions.title: Workstream entity identifier prefix and format: WS, two-letter form acceptable without locking the cross-spec norm
decisions.context: The workstream entity is the first of six new governance entities designed in the governance entity schema-design workstream. Its identifier prefix sets a small precedent for the cross-spec collision list and contributes to the visible-or-not pattern of cross-spec consistency on prefix length. The existing V2 collision list spans two-letter (PI), three-letter (DEC, SES, RSK, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM, ENT), and four-letter (PROC, CRMC) prefixes; the governance schema spec guide section 6 admits 2-5 letters without locking a length. The remaining five governance entities have working prefixes spanning two to four letters (CONV, RB, WT, COP, DEP).
decisions.decision: **Adopt `WS` as the workstream identifier prefix, format `WS-NNN` zero-padded to three digits, server-assigned on POST omission with helper at GET /workstreams/next-identifier.** The two-letter form is affirmed as acceptable; the cross-spec norm is NOT locked at two letters — downstream conversations may choose three or four letters where the two-letter form would be ambiguous or read awkwardly. Spec guide section 6's collision list is extended with WS.
decisions.rationale: WS is short, mnemonic, and easy to scan. The two-letter form matches PI's existing posture. The collision list has no conflict with WS. The spec guide's 2-5 letter range admits the choice without justification. Locking the cross-spec norm at two letters would constrain the five downstream conversations needlessly; each conversation has its own context for choosing length.
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option (b) — WST, WSTRM, or WORK.** Longer forms; no semantic improvement; would set a soft precedent for three-or-more-letter prefixes that the remaining five conversations might not want to inherit. Rejected because there is no functional reason to prefer length.
decisions.consequences: Spec guide section 6 collision list extended with WS. The remaining five governance entity conversations have first refusal on their own prefix choices, with no cross-spec length norm locked at this conversation. The workstream entity's spec section 3.1 documents the soft-prefix-length posture explicitly for downstream readers.

decisions.title: Workstream-to-conversation relationship via references-table edge per DEC-120, with new relationship kind conversation_belongs_to_workstream
decisions.context: DEC-120's decision text states explicitly: 'Conversations belong to a workstream via a relationship in the references table.' The kickoff prompt for SES-048 carried a working assumption that the workstream-to-conversation relationship would be a direct foreign-key column on the conversation table (conversation_workstream_id). The discrepancy needed to be resolved at the workstream entity's schema-design conversation — both because the workstream spec must describe how the relationship is modelled and because the choice sets a precedent for analogous parent-child relationships across the remaining five governance entities (close_out_payload to conversation, work_ticket to conversation, deposit_event to close_out_payload, and so on).
decisions.decision: **Adopt the references-table edge mechanism per DEC-120's binding decision text.** The workstream-to-conversation relationship is modelled as a references-table edge with new relationship kind `conversation_belongs_to_workstream` (source-first per DEC-048: source = conversation, target = workstream). No `workstream_id` column on the conversation table. The access layer enforces 'every conversation has exactly one outgoing conversation_belongs_to_workstream edge'; POSTs that omit the edge or supply multiple edges return HTTP 422. Vocabulary additions: REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS += conversation_belongs_to_workstream; ENTITY_TYPES += workstream, conversation; matching clause in _kinds_for_pair. Cross-spec precedent locked: references-edge over foreign-key for parent-child governance relationships across the six new entities.
decisions.rationale: DEC-120's text is binding — foundation decisions are overridden only with clear cause, and the kickoff's preference for foreign-key offered convenience rather than cause. The references-edge posture matches the governance entity family's coherent architectural shape: parent-child relationships across the six new entities all use the same mechanism, making the family easier to reason about per type and at use time. The query and schema-enforcement costs of references-edge over foreign-key are real but small at expected scale (10-20 conversations per workstream, dozens of workstreams).
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option (b) — Direct foreign-key column `conversation_workstream_id` on conversation table.** Would give a single indexed filter for 'list conversations in a workstream' and schema-level NOT NULL enforcement of the parent relationship. Rejected because DEC-120's decision text explicitly chose references-table edge, and the query/enforcement gains do not constitute cause to override a foundation decision. Methodology workstream's mixed-mechanism pattern (entity_scopes_to_domain as references-edge, process_belongs_to_domain as foreign-key) was considered as precedent for case-by-case mechanism choice; rejected for the governance family because architectural consistency across the family outweighs per-relationship optimization.
decisions.consequences: All five remaining governance entity schema-design conversations inherit references-edge for analogous parent-child relationships unless they have specific cause to deviate. One new relationship_kind to register in vocab.py; matching Alembic migration on refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint. Access-layer rule enforcing exactly-one parent edge per conversation; tests required. The references-table generic supersedes/is_about/references kinds continue to sit alongside the new typed kinds in the universal references table.

decisions.title: Workstream lifecycle: five statuses (planned, in_flight, complete, cancelled, superseded) with truly terminal terminals and supersession-requires-edge rule
decisions.context: DEC-120's decision text names four statuses: planned, in_flight, complete, cancelled. The kickoff prompt for SES-048 carried a working assumption of three statuses (planned, in_flight, complete) with cancelled and superseded flagged as open questions. DEC-117 references 'three-status lifecycle posture' as the convention inherited from the methodology workstream. Three foundation documents named different status counts; the workstream entity's schema-design conversation had to reconcile and decide the lifecycle. DEC-119's conversation entity carries seven statuses including superseded, raising the question of parity between workstream and conversation.
decisions.decision: **Adopt five statuses: `planned`, `in_flight`, `complete`, `cancelled`, `superseded`.** The default starter status is `planned`. Transitions per spec section 3.4.1: planned admits in_flight, cancelled, superseded; in_flight admits complete, cancelled, superseded; the three terminal states (complete, cancelled, superseded) admit no outgoing transitions — terminal means truly terminal. Access-layer rule: setting workstream_status to `superseded` requires an outgoing `supersedes` reference edge to another workstream record; POSTs and PATCHes that violate the rule return HTTP 422 with `supersession_requires_successor_edge`. The supersession edge reuses the existing generic `supersedes` reference kind (already valid for the (workstream, workstream) pair via _kinds_for_pair's source_type == target_type rule once workstream is in ENTITY_TYPES); no new kind is introduced for the supersession relationship. Cross-spec precedent locked: truly terminal terminal states for workflow-shaped governance lifecycles.
decisions.rationale: DEC-120's text binds cancelled (already named in the foundation decision); the kickoff's three-status framing drifted and is corrected. Adding superseded as a fifth status provides parity with DEC-119's conversation entity, where superseded is one of seven statuses; the project's operating history demonstrates supersession as a real workflow event (the methodology workstream effectively superseded an originally-planned user-interface version 0.4 kickoff). Truly terminal terminal states preserve timeline semantics — reactivation of a finished workstream blurs the timeline; a workstream that resumes is conceptually a new workstream with an inbound supersedes edge. Reusing the existing generic supersedes kind avoids a redundant typed kind for what is already a well-established cross-entity semantic.
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option (b) — Four statuses with no superseded.** Supersession via existing supersedes reference edge with terminal status remaining `cancelled`. Rejected because the asymmetry with conversation's superseded status (DEC-119) would invite confusion at the family level. **Option (c) — Reactivatable terminal states (e.g., complete → in_flight on resume).** Rejected because workflow-shape lifecycles work best with terminal-means-terminal discipline; resumption is more accurately modelled as a new workstream record that supersedes the prior one. **Option (d) — Introduce a new typed kind `workstream_supersedes_workstream`.** Rejected as redundant with the existing generic supersedes kind whose semantic is identical.
decisions.consequences: New vocabulary in vocab.py: WORKSTREAM_STATUSES = frozenset({planned, in_flight, complete, cancelled, superseded}); WORKSTREAM_STATUS_TRANSITIONS dict with all three terminals as empty-successor sets. Access-layer rule enforcing supersession-requires-edge; tests for both the create-with-superseded-status case and the patch-to-superseded case. Cross-spec precedent of truly terminal terminal states for workflow-shaped governance lifecycles; conversation entity inherits it for its seven-state lifecycle. Cross-spec precedent that lifecycles richer than three states are acceptable for governance entities — the spec guide already endorsed this; this decision exercises it.

decisions.title: Workstream field inventory with per-status lifecycle timestamps for the workflow-shaped lifecycle
decisions.context: Workstream needed a field inventory under minimum-viable scope: enough to host the organizing-unit concept faithfully without over-engineering. The kickoff prompt named several candidate fields (purpose, description, target user-interface version, master plan link, outcome summary) as working assumptions. The methodology entities (domain, entity, process, crm_candidate) carry only base timestamps (created_at, updated_at, deleted_at) and rely on change_log for status-transition history; the kickoff prompt's working assumption for workstream included per-status lifecycle timestamps (planned_at, started_at, completed_at). The divergence between the methodology pattern and the kickoff's per-status pattern needed an explicit decision since it sets a cross-spec precedent for downstream timeline-shaped entities.
decisions.decision: **Adopt a nine-field schema plus three inherited base timestamps plus four per-status lifecycle timestamps.** Identity fields: workstream_identifier, workstream_name. Content fields: workstream_purpose (required, one-sentence), workstream_description (required, paragraph), workstream_notes (optional, internal). Classification fields: workstream_status. Relationship fields: NONE (parent-child relationships go through refs per DEC-124; no nesting per DEC-127). Timestamp fields: inherited workstream_created_at, workstream_updated_at, workstream_deleted_at, plus four nullable lifecycle timestamps server-set on the matching transition — workstream_started_at (planned → in_flight), workstream_completed_at (→ complete), workstream_cancelled_at (→ cancelled), workstream_superseded_at (→ superseded). No workstream_planned_at column (redundant with workstream_created_at since planned is the default starter). The three terminal-state timestamps are mutually exclusive on terminal records. No storage-level length caps on text fields. Cross-spec precedent locked: per-status lifecycle timestamps for workflow-shaped governance lifecycles.
decisions.rationale: Workflow-shaped lifecycles benefit from per-status timestamps in a way propose-verify lifecycles don't. Queries like 'average time-to-complete' and 'workstreams that stalled at planned' are first-class under per-status timestamps and indirect joins through change_log under the methodology base-only pattern. Storage cost of four extra nullable columns is negligible. The methodology divergence is appropriate because methodology entities use propose-verify lifecycles (candidate, confirmed, deferred) where per-status timestamps add no value; workstream's lifecycle is different in kind. Deliberately omitted fields (target user-interface version, outcome summary) have weak justifications in the minimum-viable shape: the build-planning conversation's session record carries the outcome story; the build-planning conversation sets the target version; both can grow into the schema additively in a later release.
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option (b) — No lifecycle timestamps (methodology parity).** Rely on change_log for transition history. Rejected because workstream's workflow-shape lifecycle has queryable per-status durations as a real use case, and methodology parity does not justify ignoring the use case when the methodology lifecycle is different in kind. **Option (c) — Single `state_changed_at` timestamp.** Tracks the last transition only. Rejected as adding nothing over updated_at and change_log together. **Option (d) — Outcome summary field as required-on-complete.** Rejected because the build-planning conversation's session record carries the outcome narrative and a duplicate field invites drift.
decisions.consequences: Workstream table has 13 columns (9 substantive + 4 inherited/lifecycle). Server-side enforcement of lifecycle-timestamp population on transitions; tests required. Cross-spec precedent of per-status lifecycle timestamps; conversation entity (with seven states per DEC-119) most likely inherits this. Deposit_event probably just needs applied_at (append-only). Future-release additions (target user-interface version, outcome summary, predecessor-successor reference kinds between workstreams, nested-workstream retrofit) are documented as deferred in spec section 3.8.3.

decisions.title: Workstream flat catalog, no nesting in this release; retrofit path is references-edge addition not self-FK column
decisions.context: DEC-120 explicitly deferred the nested-workstream question to 'the workstream entity's own schema-design conversation,' phrasing the deferral as 'optional parent-workstream reference if nested workstreams are admitted.' The workstream entity's spec needed to resolve whether workstream is a flat catalog or a tree, and if a tree, what mechanism (self-referential foreign key like topic.parent_topic, or references-table edge with a new parent-child kind). The choice sets a soft precedent for downstream governance entities considering hierarchy and informs the Cleveland Business Mentors Planning Item 001 sub-domain hierarchy question on the methodology domain entity, though it does not pre-decide PI-001.
decisions.decision: **Adopt a flat catalog — no nesting in this release.** Workstream records are siblings. Multi-stage initiatives are expressed via free text in workstream_description or via future predecessor/successor reference kinds (not modelled in this release). The retrofit path, if nesting becomes a real need in a future release, is a references-edge addition with a new relationship kind (provisional name `workstream_parent_of_workstream`) plus an access-layer 'at most one parent edge per workstream' rule plus user-interface tree rendering. The retrofit deliberately uses references-edge rather than self-referential foreign-key so the workstream table is not retroactively modified.
decisions.rationale: Zero observed need from the project's operating history — all workstreams to date have been flat siblings. DEC-120's 'if nested workstreams are admitted' phrasing leans toward not-admitting. User-interface cost of hierarchical rendering is real (tree widget, expand-collapse state, breadcrumbs, drag-to-reparent); deferring until signal supports it is the minimum-viable posture. The project's actual multi-stage organizing pattern is sequential (predecessor/successor relationships between workstreams) rather than nested (parent/child trees); sequential edges would model the real pattern more accurately than nesting if added later.
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option (b) — Self-referential foreign-key column workstream_parent_id (topic.parent_topic pattern).** Rejected because the future retrofit would touch the workstream table; references-edge is a lighter retrofit. **Option (c) — References-edge with new parent-child kind in this release.** Rejected because no real need exists and adding an unused mechanism creates dead vocabulary that the build-planning conversation must aggregate without justification.
decisions.consequences: No nesting infrastructure in this release. Soft precedent for downstream governance entities considering hierarchy: prefer flat unless real need surfaces; if hierarchy is added later, prefer references-edge retrofit over self-FK column addition. Cleveland Business Mentors Planning Item 001 (sub-domain hierarchy on the methodology domain entity) is NOT pre-decided by this decision — that conversation makes its own call with this reasoning available but not binding. Future workstream-shaped initiatives expressing multi-stage organization use free text or (when needed) future predecessor/successor reference kinds rather than nesting.

decisions.title: Workstream master plan linkage via references-edge, standard API and UI defaults, soft-delete posture, sixteen acceptance criteria
decisions.context: Routine items batched together at the close of the workstream schema-design conversation: how to model the master-plan-to-workstream linkage, what API endpoints the entity exposes, what the user-interface looks like, soft-delete versus append-only posture, and how many testable acceptance criteria the spec captures. Each item follows defaults from the schema-spec methodology guide; no deviations were warranted, but the choices set precedents and need to be recorded.
decisions.decision: **(a) Master plan linkage** via references-edge with new relationship kind `workstream_planned_in_reference_book` (source = workstream, target = reference_book; cardinality zero-or-one from workstream side; provisional naming, reference_book conversation may refine, build-planning reconciles). ENTITY_TYPES gains reference_book for vocab registration. **(b) Supersession edge** reuses the existing generic `supersedes` kind for the (workstream, workstream) pair; no new typed kind. **(c) API surface** is the standard 8-endpoint set per spec guide section 3.5 with no deviations: GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/restore/next-identifier; all endpoints return the {data, meta, errors} envelope. **(d) User-interface** uses the default ListDetailPanel layout per spec guide section 3.6, with two natural additions documented in the spec — conditional lifecycle-timestamp rendering on the detail pane (showing only non-null timestamps) and supersession-requires-edge UX (the user must add the outgoing supersedes edge before committing status to superseded). **(e) Soft-delete** posture is the default — soft-delete with restore — explicitly distinct from cancelled status (administrative housekeeping versus lifecycle outcome). **(f) Acceptance criteria** capture sixteen testable statements in spec section 3.7 (gate requires ten).
decisions.rationale: Each item follows the spec guide's defaults with reasoning in the spec sections cited. No deviations warranted at this scope. The master-plan kind name workstream_planned_in_reference_book is provisional because the reference_book conversation (third in the workstream order) has not run yet; the kind name may be refined for verb-tense consistency with reference_book's other inbound kinds, and build-planning reconciles. Soft-delete fits workstream because the entity is organizing metadata not transactional fact (append-only fits decisions, sessions, deposit_events; not workstream).
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Master plan as a foreign-key column workstream_master_plan_reference_book_id** — rejected per Decision 124's references-edge-over-foreign-key precedent. **Append-only posture for workstream** — rejected because workstream records can be created in error or with the wrong name; correction via edit-or-delete-and-restore is the appropriate posture. **Outcome summary as a required-on-complete field** — already rejected in DEC-126. **Tree-widget sidebar instead of flat panel** — out of scope per DEC-127 (no nesting in this release).
decisions.consequences: Vocabulary additions to aggregate at build-planning: REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS += workstream_planned_in_reference_book; ENTITY_TYPES += reference_book; matching _kinds_for_pair clause for (workstream, reference_book). API endpoint test coverage required across all eight endpoints. Default UI layout with the two natural additions reduces to the existing EntityCrudDialog and ReferencesSection widgets per UI version 0.3 patterns. Sixteen acceptance criteria translate into specific test cases at build-planning time. The spec's per-spec completeness gate (spec guide section 7.1) passes: all nine subsections of section 3 substantively filled, at least ten acceptance criteria, explicit category tags on open questions, cross-references include workstream plan, spec guide, and foundation decisions.
